title: Tulotis ussuriensis
taxonomy: Tulotis ussuriensis is classified under the family Fabaceae and is a type of flowering plant. It belongs to the genus Tulotis and is native to East Asia. Further classification shows that it is an herbaceous perennial legume. The plant's classification also places it within the order Fabales. More specifically, it is part of the subfamily Faboideae.

intro: Tulotis ussuriensis, also known as Ussuri Tulotis, is a type of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, native to East Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to lower blood pressure and alleviate symptoms of hypertension due to its vasodilatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Tulotis ussuriensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, decoctions, and extracts, which are often used to treat cardiovascular and respiratory conditions.
